summary: A solid matrix mix captures a fire‑filled 2/8/70 show highlighted by a blistering Smokestack Lightning opener, soaring Dark Star and an epic Lovelight.
review: The performance is widely praised – the band rolls out a thunderous Smokestack Lightning, a majestic Dark Star, tight St. Stephen > Not Fade Away sandwiches and a sprawling 35‑minute Love Light that many call one of the era’s best. The recording is a composite of soundboard and audience sources; it sounds better than average with good overall balance but suffers from a slightly low drum mix, occasional tinny/phased artifacts and uneven levels in the middle section.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is consistently praised (many standout songs, strong second set) while recording quality is described as good but with notable flaws; weighted average yields a ~4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience source praised, soundboard sections clear. Mix slightly low on drums, occasional tinny/phased character and balance issues in the middle of the set.

standout_songs: St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > St. Stephen
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Sitting On Top of the World
show_quality.setlist_flow: A high‑energy opener with Smokestack Lightning sets the tone, the first set flows through solid roots (Morning Dew, China Cat), and the second set builds to a peak with Dark Star, the Stephen/NFA sandwich and an expansive Love Light that carries the show to a triumphant close.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is highlighted throughout – crisp tone on Smokestack, soaring leads in Dark Star and high‑part harmonies on China Cat Sunflower.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir’s vocal/guitar interplay with Garcia is repeatedly praised, especially during the second‑set jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums are noted as a bit low in the mix and occasionally muddied by the composite source.
